author: campersurfer
title: Review issue identity structurally unstable — sha256(summary) in issue IDs causes phantom churn
notes: All claims verified: sha256(summary)[:8] is baked into issue IDs at per_file.py:113 and holistic_issue_flow.py:107. make_issue uses name (containing the hash) as part of issue_id (filtering.py:160). Auto-resolve (resolution.py:29) marks old IDs as stale when not in new_ids, so any summary rewording by the LLM creates a new issue and resolves the old one — losing first_seen, reopen_count, note, and suppression state. The identifier field alone would provide stable dedup. No duplicates found.
